title: store: migration v1→v2 re-reads schema_version outside transaction, creating TOCTOU
description: store.rs runs the v0→v1 migration in a transaction (tx.commit()), then for v1→v2 the code re-reads PRAGMA user_version with a fresh SELECT outside any transaction. If two relay instances start simultaneously against the same SQLite file (rare but possible), both could read sv=1, both enter the if sv < 2 block, and both execute the DDL. SQLite's DDL is not transactional for CREATE TABLE and ALTER TABLE in the same way — the second instance will get an error on 'CREATE TABLE group_members_v2' (already exists). The error would bubble up from Store::new() causing the second instance to crash at startup. Not a data-corruption risk, but a startup-race availability issue.
